title: UMI Image Layered World Model
description: UMI Image Layered World Model: build tri-view robot video world modeling, decompose videos into robot / object-contact / scene layers, and prove layered data improves manipulation.
summary: Build synchronized tri-view robot video world models, decompose real/generated clips into recomposable robot/object-contact/occluder/scene layers, and verify that those layers improve IDM, pose, reward/eval, background replacement, and downstream policies. The project card keeps stable architecture only; dated experiment logs live on the linked TODO comments.

subprojects.label: A
subprojects.title: UMI Ego World
subprojects.body: Stage 1: generate synchronized action-conditioned robot videos from left/right wrist plus head/fixed views. References: Gamma-World, COMBO, Solaris for shared multi-view context. Baselines: Wan2.1 dual-wrist, Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Diffusers, nvidia/Cosmos3-Nano-Policy-DROID.
subprojects.output: shared-context robot video data + view/action consistency gates

subprojects.label: B
subprojects.title: Robot Video Layers
subprojects.body: Stage 2: build mask-first robot/object-contact/occluder/scene layers from real or generated clips. Route: SAM3/MaskWAM-style annotation loop, RoboTwin synthetic masks, VACE masked-V2V completion, and SuperSVG/LIVE/SAM-contour sidecars; Qwen/RevealLayer stay as negative references.
subprojects.output: mask.png + RGBA/video layers + inpainted background + SVG/contact sidecars + layer_manifest + recomposition QA

subprojects.label: C
subprojects.title: Layered Data Utility
subprojects.body: Stage 3: run utility ablations against flat RGB and mask-only inputs. Use robot layers for IDM/pose/VO, object-contact layers for reward and 6D pose, and scene layers for background/domain adaptation.
subprojects.output: FoundationPose / IDM / reward / policy gain evidence

asset_alt: Shared-Backbone Dual-Branch Wan architecture diagram for UMI, showing left and right gripper camera histories, shared video VAE/tokenizer, condition encoder, view embeddings, shared Wan backbone, dual prediction heads, and training objectives.
asset_caption: Shared-Backbone Dual-Branch Wan: UMI Stage 1 architecture for separate left/right wrist video latents, view embeddings, shared Wan2.1 backbone, dual decoded outputs, and cross-view / temporal / state-action consistency losses.
details: Claim: Image-Layered Robot Data should improve manipulation only if it helps downstream robot learning, not merely because the decomposed images look clean.
Stage 1 acceptance: multiple generated robot-camera videos must share overlapping scene, object, contact, and action consequences instead of drifting as independent clips.

Stage 2 acceptance: every layer batch must preserve raster truth and structure: masks, RGBA/video, inpainted background, optional SVG/contact metadata, layer_manifest.json, temporal consistency, and recomposition QA.
Stage 3 acceptance: compare flat RGB, mask-only/SAM, and full layered data on the same tasks before claiming gains in IDM, FoundationPose, reward/eval, background replacement, or policy training.
Current open execution gates: VACE raster video inpainting, global layered-dataset route selection, and FoundationPose/object-pose utility testing.

Dataset substrate: Hoi! is the selected tri-view / multi ego-view replacement candidate because it provides synchronized head-ego, manipulator-centric ego, and fixed external views; MV-UMI and EgoMimic remain references/backups.

references.title: Gamma-World: Generative Multi-Agent World Modeling Beyond Two Players
references.notes: MAIN REFERENCE / Stage 1 build-upon route. Gamma-World defines the synchronized action-conditioned interactive world-model lane, with Simplex Rotary Agent Encoding, Sparse Hub Attention, and causal real-time rollout. Use it as the Stage 1 reference for UMI Ego World generation and shared-context multi-view rollout; then make the UMI contribution about robot-data utility, view/layer/eval contracts, IDM/control decoding, and policy ablations rather than agent-count scaling.

references.title: COMBO: Compositional World Models for Embodied Multi-Agent Cooperation
references.notes: Stage 1 shared-context reference. COMBO estimates overall world state from partial egocentric RGBD observations, factorizes multi-agent joint actions into compositional action conditions, and uses the resulting world model with VLM-assisted action proposal / intent tracking / outcome evaluation for online cooperation. Use it to frame UMI left/right/head views as partial observations of one latent scene, not independent video targets.

references.title: Solaris: Building a Multiplayer Video World Model in Minecraft
references.notes: Stage 1 multi-view consistency reference. Solaris builds a synchronized multiplayer video+action data system, trains through staged single-player to multiplayer modeling with causal and Self Forcing variants, introduces Checkpointed Self Forcing for longer-horizon supervision, and evaluates movement, memory, grounding, building, and view consistency. Use it as the strongest analogy for UMI's multiple generated camera streams sharing the same world state.

references.title: Dashboard asset: world-model taxonomy
references.notes: User-provided taxonomy figure. Keep as a visual guardrail for Tri-View: reconstruction models, next-step predictive models, and runnable simulators are different world-model claims and need different evaluation evidence.

references.title: Qwen-Image-Layered: Layered Decomposition for Inherent Editability
references.notes: NEGATIVE BASELINE / passed over for current robot-layer pipeline. Group tests on 2026-06-17/18 showed Qwen-Image-Layered can control layer count but cannot bind required robot/object/background semantics to each layer; prompt constraints act like captions, producing merged entities, residual/shadow/support layers, failed pure-background rendering, and weak inpainting. Keep as evidence that generic editable layers are insufficient for robot manipulation.

references.title: RevealLayer: Disentangling Hidden and Visible Layers via Occlusion-Aware Image Decomposition
references.notes: REFERENCE ONLY / not the short-term practical route after group testing. RevealLayer's occlusion-aware RGBA formulation remains useful background, but the current robot-scene probe was judged unusable. Do not depend on RevealLayer as the Stage 2 mainline unless new robot-specific guidance or code evidence changes this result.

references.title: ObjectForesight: Predicting Future 3D Object Trajectories from Human Videos
references.notes: MAIN REFERENCE / object-centric dynamics route. ObjectForesight predicts future 6-DoF object trajectories from short egocentric video histories and builds large-scale pseudo-ground-truth object trajectory data from human videos using segmentation, tracking, reconstruction, depth/camera motion, and pose estimation. Use as the reference for object/contact layer targets and for the segment+track+reconstruct data pipeline.

references.title: Walk through Paintings: Egocentric World Models from Internet Priors
references.notes: Support reference for Ego World: transforms pretrained video diffusion models into action-conditioned egocentric world models with motor-command conditioning and structural consistency evaluation.

references.title: LOME: Learning Human-Object Manipulation with Action-Conditioned Egocentric World Model
references.notes: Support reference for action-conditioned egocentric human-object interaction video: uses reference image, text prompt, and per-frame actions to generate physically plausible manipulation videos.

references.title: EgoSim: Egocentric World Simulator for Embodied Interaction Generation
references.notes: Support reference for continuously updated egocentric interaction simulation with 3D state updating, geometry-action-aware observation simulation, and scalable egocentric video data construction.

references.title: EVA: Aligning Video World Models with Executable Robot Actions via Inverse Dynamics Rewards
references.notes: Support reference for IDM: generated video rollouts can be decoded into actions by an inverse dynamics model, and IDM-derived rewards can align video generation with executable robot constraints.

references.title: RoboEngine: Plug-and-Play Robot Data Augmentation
references.notes: Background-layer / scene-adaptation reference. RoboEngine uses semantic robot segmentation and background generation for physics- and task-aware robot data augmentation, matching the scene-layer domain-randomization track.

references.title: Single-View Robot Pose and Joint Angle Estimation via Render & Compare
references.notes: Support reference for visible-robot branch: RoboPose estimates robot joint angles and 6D camera-to-robot pose from a single RGB image of a known articulated robot.

references.title: ORB-SLAM3: An Accurate Open-Source Library for Visual, Visual-Inertial and Multi-Map SLAM
references.notes: Support reference for no-arm-visible branch: visual/visual-inertial SLAM supports monocular, stereo, RGB-D, pinhole, and fisheye cameras, suitable as a VO/SLAM baseline for wrist/head ego views.

references.title: VGGT: Visual Geometry Grounded Transformer
references.notes: Support reference for feed-forward visual geometry: predicts camera parameters, depth, point maps, and 3D point tracks from one or many views, useful for wrist/head egomotion and object-scene geometry.

references.title: SANA-WM: Efficient Minute-Scale World Modeling with Hybrid Linear Diffusion Transformer
references.notes: Reference for efficient minute-scale world modeling, hybrid linear attention, precise 6-DoF camera control, and metric pose-supervised long-video generation.

references.title: iWorld-Bench: A Benchmark for Interactive World Models with a Unified Action Generation Framework
references.notes: Reference for evaluating interactive/camera-controllable world models with packaged metadata, camera trajectories, action-control, memory-ability, camera-following, and all-metric evaluation wrappers.

references.title: EgoX: Egocentric Video Generation from a Single Exocentric Video
references.notes: Exo-to-ego reference: point-cloud rendered ego prior, width/channel latent concatenation, geometry-guided self-attention, LoRA on Wan 2.1 14B; useful for cross-view conditioning design.

references.title: Intention-driven Ego-to-Exo Video Generation
references.notes: Ego-to-exo reference: uses view-independent intention, head trajectory, action description, optical flow and occlusion maps to synthesize exocentric video from egocentric input.

references.title: WorldWander: Bridging Egocentric and Exocentric Worlds in Video Generation
references.notes: Bidirectional ego/exo translation reference with in-context perspective alignment and synchronized ego-exo triplets; relevant for tri-view cross-view synchronization.

references.title: MolmoAct: Action Reasoning Models that can Reason in Space
references.notes: Dataset-design reference for this project, not a direct substitute for our missing view: MolmoAct's real-robot dataset uses two side-mounted views and one wrist camera, converts episodes into side+wrist paired examples, and adds depth/trace/action-reasoning supervision.

references.title: MolmoAct2-BimanualYAM Dataset
references.notes: LeRobot-format schema reference for synchronized bimanual manipulation data: camera streams are observation.images.top / left / right at 360x640 30fps, with 14D bimanual state/action. Useful for dataset packaging and ablations, but it does not resolve the current UMI/Jianzhi missing fixed robot-ego view.

references.title: Hoi!: A Multimodal Dataset for Force-Grounded, Cross-View Articulated Manipulation
references.notes: Selected dataset replacement substrate for UMI Image Layered World Model on 2026-06-17. Public Hoi! bundles cover synchronized head-ego, manipulator-centric ego / UMI-style, wrist, and fixed external views, making it the best available open data surface for shared-context multi ego-view manifests.

references.title: Dr. Robot: Differentiable Robot Rendering
references.notes: Robot-layer / IDM reference. Dr. Robot makes robot appearance differentiable with respect to control parameters, so it is a natural reference for whole-arm pose/joint recovery and for replacing a generic IDM with robot-kinematic visual gradients.

references.title: ORB-SLAM3: Visual, Visual-Inertial and Multi-Map SLAM
references.notes: Wrist/head ego geometry baseline. ORB-SLAM3 supports visual and visual-inertial SLAM with monocular, stereo, RGB-D, pinhole, and fisheye setups, which matches the need to recover wrist-camera motion when robot pixels are incomplete or absent.

references.title: LIV: Language-Image Representations and Rewards for Robotic Control
references.notes: Object-layer reward reference. LIV pretrains control-centric visual-language representations from human video without action labels and can produce language-conditioned rewards on human and robot videos, which maps to object/contact process reward.

references.title: ARRO: Augmented Reality for Robots
references.notes: Policy-utility evidence. ARRO shows that segmentation-driven augmented robot observations can improve Diffusion Policy and generalist robot policies; this supports the stronger ablation that full Image-Layered Robot Data should help manipulation.

references.title: 10Kh RealOmni-Open DataSet
references.notes: New Stage 1 data source from the UMI model-structure update. GenRobot describes 10Kh RealOmni-Open as real-world, diverse-skill embodied data with 1M+ data clips and ongoing expansion; use it for the dual-wrist Wan2.1 baseline only after checking view/camera availability, download access, license, task coverage, and annotation fields.

references.title: Wan2.2 MoE high-noise / low-noise architecture
references.notes: Training-risk reference for UMI: Wan2.2-A14B uses a two-expert denoising MoE, with a high-noise expert for early/layout steps and a low-noise expert for later/detail steps. This improves quality but complicates custom finetuning because UMI adaptation must decide whether and how to train each expert and where to switch by SNR/timestep.

references.title: Wan2.1 LoRA fine-tuning training guide
references.notes: Implementation reference for the first UMI training route: provides a complete Wan2.1 LoRA workflow covering dataset structure, metadata.json, model download, DeepSpeed/FSDP training, validation, and inference testing.

references.title: Finetrainers Wan training support
references.notes: Implementation reference: finetrainers documents LoRA and full-finetune support and lists tested Wan2.1 T2V/I2V/FLF2V checkpoints. Use as a cleaner baseline toolchain candidate before modifying dual-wrist inputs.

references.title: VACE: All-in-One Video Creation and Editing
references.notes: Video inpainting / masked V2V candidate for Stage 2. Official VACE repo describes reference-to-video, video-to-video editing, masked video-to-video editing, and free composition, making it a direct fit for mask-tracking -> video inpaint -> recomposition QA.

references.title: Wan2.1 VACE model release
references.notes: Wan2.1 VACE release reference. Use for inference/code/weights alignment when testing VACE on robot-mask clips; keep Wan2.1 compatibility with the Stage 1 training route.

references.title: RoboTwin 2.0: scalable dual-arm manipulation data generator and benchmark
references.notes: Candidate mask-rich synthetic data source for Stage 2. RoboTwin 2.0 is built for bimanual manipulation data generation and benchmarking, with SAPIEN-based simulation, strong domain randomization, and a 50-task / 731-object benchmark surface. Group note: inspect whether provided grayscale masks expose per-object mask IDs directly usable for layer pseudo-labels.

references.title: RoboTwin 2.0 official repository
references.notes: Implementation/data-audit target for checking mask files, grayscale ID conventions, camera views, object metadata, and whether generated episodes can export robot/object/contact/occluder/background masks.

references.title: MaskWAM: Unifying Mask Prompting and Prediction for World-Action Models
references.notes: Comparison paper for the mask-centric WAM direction. MaskWAM integrates masks as explicit inputs and future predictions via a unified Mixture of Transformers, addressing spatial ambiguity and semantic grounding. For UMI, borrow the annotation loop and future-mask-prediction lesson, but keep the contribution as reusable robot/object-contact/occluder/scene RGBA/video layers with inpaint, SVG/contact sidecars, recomposition QA, and IDM/reward/FoundationPose utility.

references.title: MaskWAM annotation pipeline figure
references.notes: Dashboard copy of the MaskWAM data-processing figure: language annotation and VLM object extraction initialize SAM3 mask sequences, human point prompts correct ambiguous rounds, and accepted masks become labeled episodes. Use as a practical reference for the global layered dataset annotation loop.

references.title: MaskWAM vs UMI memo + v0 dataset plan (private backup)
references.notes: Moved to PRIVATE BorisGuo6/Gamma-World repo (robot-reproduction branch, docs/research/): MaskWAM-vs-UMI comparison memo + global Image-Layered dataset v0 plan (method matrix / IO schema / cost / QA / utility / failures / next-batch). Not published on the public site. Core distinction: MaskWAM=mask-as-policy-supervision; UMI=layers-as-reusable-data-infrastructure.

risks_decisions: Keep one research card with three stages: UMI Ego World, Robot Video Layers, and Layered Data Utility.
Robot layer is not only a mask: it must carry enough temporal/kinematic signal for pose, state, inverse dynamics, or wrist-camera egomotion.
Object/contact layer is the reward/process layer: preserve identity, contact, 6D motion, and task progress rather than foreground pixels only.
Background/scene layer is an adaptation handle: background replacement must not corrupt robot/object labels or dynamics.
Qwen Image-Layered and RevealLayer are negative/direct-layer references for current robot scenes; the practical v0 route is mask tracking plus inpaint/VACE plus SVG sidecars.
MaskWAM is useful as a VLM -> SAM3 -> human-verification annotation loop, but UMI is not mask-only WAM; UMI persists recomposable raster/video layers and contact/SVG metadata.
AutoDL training is single-node only for this effort; plan around at most one 8-GPU node unless a separate multi-node cluster is provisioned.
Do not wait for a perfect end-to-end layer generator before utility testing; use the best available semi-automatic layered clips for FoundationPose, IDM, reward/eval, and recomposition first.

Stage 1 action-conditioning must pass a base-quality gate before action sensitivity is meaningful: trained outputs should preserve base visual structure before zero/time/wrong-row controls are interpreted.

timeline.target_note: Paper target for the tri-view layered manipulation world model; schedule should be updated once the exact submission plan is fixed.

milestones.title: Merge Image-Layered WM into UMI tri-view layered world-model card
Tri-view data schema and layer/eval ablation spec ready
